de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.communication

Class SimplePipe

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.communication.SimplePipe
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Pipe

    public class SimplePipe
extends java.lang.Object
implements Pipe
    This class represents a simplified version of the existing pipe. It assumes that the external process merges stderr and stdout (see ExternalProcessLauncher).

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      SimplePipe(java.io.InputStream input, java.lang.String[] messageDelimiters, java.io.OutputStream output, SolverCommunication session, java.lang.Process process)
      Creates a new SimplePipe.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void close()
      Forcibly closes the Pipe by destroying the process.
      SolverCommunication getSolverCommunication()
      Can be used to obtain the messages sent to and from the solver as well as its final result.
      java.lang.String readMessage()
      Reads a message from the external process.
      void sendMessage(java.lang.String message)
      Sends a message to the external process the Pipe is connected to.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • SimplePipe

        public SimplePipe(@Nonnull
                  java.io.InputStream input,
                  @Nonnull
                  java.lang.String[] messageDelimiters,
                  @Nonnull
                  java.io.OutputStream output,
                  @Nonnull
                  SolverCommunication session,
                  @Nonnull
                  java.lang.Process process)
        Creates a new SimplePipe.
        Parameters:
        input - the InputStream connected to the merged stdout and stderr of the external process
        messageDelimiters - the delimiters which separate one message from another
        output - the OutputStream connected to stdin of the external process
        session - the message list where to log the messages to
        process - the external process this pipe is connected to

    • Method Detail

      • sendMessage

        public void sendMessage(@Nonnull
                        java.lang.String message)
                 throws java.io.IOException
        Description copied from interface: Pipe
        Sends a message to the external process the Pipe is connected to.
        Specified by:
        sendMessage in interface Pipe
        Parameters:
        message - the message to send
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • readMessage

        @Nullable
public java.lang.String readMessage()
                                       throws java.io.IOException,
                                              java.lang.InterruptedException
        Description copied from interface: Pipe
        Reads a message from the external process. This method blocks until there is a further message or the underlying stream has been closed.
        Specified by:
        readMessage in interface Pipe
        Returns:
        the received message
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if reading fails
        java.lang.InterruptedException - if interrupted while waiting

      • getSolverCommunication

        @Nonnull
public SolverCommunication getSolverCommunication()
        Description copied from interface: Pipe
        Can be used to obtain the messages sent to and from the solver as well as its final result.
        Specified by:
        getSolverCommunication in interface Pipe
        Returns:
        the data sent between KeY and solver until now

      • close

        public void close()
        Description copied from interface: Pipe
        Forcibly closes the Pipe by destroying the process. Calling this method should be avoided if possible, since the Pipe may lose messages otherwise. Most likely it is better to make the process terminate normally (by sending "(exit)" to the SMT solver) and wait for the pipe to automatically close.
        Specified by:
        close in interface Pipe
